ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn does not have a specific entry for salmon grain bowl with egg; 'grain bowl food product' (FOODON_00002809) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_description: Mixed dish — grain-based with fish and egg
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP (composite mixed dish) used as the closest defensible category for a grain bowl containing fish, egg, and vegetable toppings. No exact FoodEx2 code exists for this specific customisable dish.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this composite grain bowl dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 350 g, representing a typical grain bowl portion as sold at Singaporean health-food and café-style outlets, comprising mixed grains, salmon, one egg, and three vegetable toppings with dressing.

nutrient_source.title: Salmon grain bowl with egg (3 toppings) — HPB Singapore Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for 'Salmon grain bowl with egg (3 toppings)' (cr_id F08010053). All core macros and secondary nutrients are sourced from this record at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data for salmon and egg; see notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — mixed grain dishes and grain bowls analogous estimate
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for salmon grain bowl with egg. GI of 45 is estimated based on analogous mixed-grain dishes (brown rice and mixed grain combinations) from the University of Sydney GI Database, which report GI values in the range 42–50 for mixed whole-grain bases. The protein and fat content from salmon and egg further attenuate postprandial glucose response. GL per serving calculated as: GI (45) × carbohydrate per serving (8.56 g/100g × 350 g serving = 29.96 g) / 100 = 13.5.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 250 mg/100g; dressing and soy-based sauces may add significant additional sodium
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should request dressing and sauce on the side to limit sodium intake, as the base dish already contributes 250 mg sodium per 100 g and typical soy-based or teriyaki dressings can add a further 200–400 mg per serving.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low estimated GI (~45), moderate carbohydrate 8.56 g/100g, high protein 6.81 g/100g, and dietary fibre 1.8 g/100g support glycaemic control
type2_diabetes.guidance: This dish is suitable for individuals with type 2 diabetes when the grain base is whole grain; the combination of low GI grains, high protein from salmon and egg, and dietary fibre moderates postprandial blood glucose, but sweet or sugar-containing dressings should be avoided.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Salmon provides EPA and DHA omega-3 fatty acids; saturated fat estimated at ~1.4 g/100g is well below the 5 g threshold; sodium is borderline at 250 mg/100g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The omega-3 fatty acids from salmon support cardiovascular health and endothelial function; individuals with cardiovascular disease should choose low-sodium dressings and confirm the grain base is whole grain to maximise fibre and micronutrient benefit.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: EPA/DHA omega-3 from salmon and antioxidants from vegetable toppings support anti-inflammatory dietary patterns
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Salmon's EPA and DHA content supports reduction of inflammatory markers in rheumatoid arthritis; patients should avoid high-sugar or chilli-based dressings that may exacerbate inflammation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Salmon grain bowl with egg (3 toppings) is a contemporary health-oriented mixed dish sold at Singaporean café-style and health-food outlets. It consists of a base of mixed cooked grains — typically a combination of brown rice, quinoa, or other whole grains — topped with a portion of cooked salmon (grilled, baked, or pan-seared), one whole egg (boiled or poached), and a choice of three vegetable or salad toppings such as edamame, cucumber, avocado, corn, or leafy greens, finished with a dressing. The dish is nutritionally balanced, providing moderate energy at approximately 118 kcal per 100 g, high-quality protein at 6.81 g/100g from both salmon and egg, healthy fats at 6.25 g/100g including omega-3 fatty acids (EPA and DHA) from salmon, and moderate carbohydrates at 8.56 g/100g with a low estimated glycaemic index of approximately 45. Dietary fibre is 1.8 g/100g and sodium is 250 mg/100g, which is borderline for hypertension management. The dish is suitable for individuals managing type 2 diabetes, cardiovascular disease, and rheumatoid arthritis due to its omega-3 content, high protein, and low GI profile. Dressing choice significantly affects sodium and sugar content and should be selected carefully by individuals with diet-sensitive conditions. The dish is classified as minimally processed and is not suitable for vegans or vegetarians due to the presence of fish and egg.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F08010053) at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data for salmon and egg, which is an approved secondary source. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for mixed-grain dishes. All other fields are completed using approved sources and defensible reasoning.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (1.4 g/100g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data for Atlantic salmon (USDA FDC ID 175167) and whole egg (USDA FDC ID 748967) weighted by approximate dish composition, as HPB does not report saturated fat for this composite dish. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this customisable composite dish are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as a composite entry as of 2026. 3. GI of 45 is an analogous estimate based on mixed whole-grain dishes from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement exists for this specific dish. 4. Halal status is marked false as it cannot be confirmed without knowledge of the specific outlet's certification and dressing ingredients. 5. Serving size of 350 g is an estimate based on typical Singaporean health-food bowl portions; actual serving size varies by outlet.
